back_translation: And it shall be, when you enter the land that the Lord your God is giving to you as an inheritance, and you possess it and dwell in it,

then you shall take from the firstfruits of every fruit of the soil that you bring from your land that the Lord your God is giving to you, and you shall put it in a basket, and you shall go to the place that the Lord your God will choose to cause His name to dwell there.

And you shall come to the priest who shall be in those days, and you shall say to him: I declare today to the Lord your God that I have come to the land that the Lord swore to our fathers to give to us.
Then the priest shall take the basket from your hand, and he shall set it before the altar of the Lord your God.

And you shall answer and say before the Lord your God: A wandering Aramean was my father, and he went down to Egypt, and he sojourned there in few persons, and he became there a great nation, mighty and numerous.
And the Egyptians did evil to us and afflicted us, and they put upon us hard labor.
And we cried out to the Lord, God of our fathers, and the Lord heard our voice, and He saw our affliction and our toil and our oppression.
And the Lord brought us out from Egypt with a strong hand and with an outstretched arm and with great terror and with signs and with wonders.
And He brought us to this place, and He gave to us this land, a land flowing with milk and honey.

And now, behold, I have brought the firstfruits of the fruit of the soil that You have given to me, O Lord. And you shall set it before the Lord your God, and you shall bow down before the Lord your God.
And you shall rejoice in all the good that the Lord your God has given to you and to your household, you and the Levite and the foreigner who is in your midst.

When you finish tithing all the tithe of your produce in the third year, the year of the tithe, then you shall give to the Levite, to the foreigner, to the orphan, and to the widow, and they shall eat in your gates and be satisfied.

And you shall say before the Lord your God: I have removed the sacred portion from the house, and also I have given it to the Levite and to the foreigner, to the orphan and to the widow, according to all Your commandment that You have commanded me; I have not transgressed from Your commandments and I have not forgotten.

I have not eaten in my mourning of it, and I have not removed of it in uncleanness, and I have not given of it for the dead; I have listened to the voice of the Lord my God; I have done according to all that You have commanded me.
Look down from Your holy dwelling, from the heavens, and bless Your people Israel and the ground that You have given to us, as You swore to our fathers, a land flowing with milk and honey.
This day the Lord your God is commanding you to do these statutes and the ordinances, and you shall keep and do them with all your heart and with all your soul.
The Lord you have declared today to be to you as God, and to walk in His ways, and to keep His statutes and His commandments and His ordinances, and to listen to His voice.
And the Lord has declared you today to be to Him as a treasured people, as He has spoken to you, and to keep all His commandments,
and to set you high above all the nations that He has made, for praise and for name and for glory, and for you to be a holy people to the Lord your God, as He has spoken.
